@graph.name: BMW models fitted with the M50 / M52 / M54

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW M50/M52/M54 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M54 typically costs £1,500–£2,500 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], varying by displacement and condition. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW M50/M52/M54 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the M50 and M52 are legendary for their robustness and simplicity. The M54 is also regarded as one of BMW's most reliable straight-six engines, with many examples exceeding 200,000 miles when properly maintained.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common BMW M50/M52/M54 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cooling system failure — plastic expansion tanks, water pumps and thermostats degrade with age, typically failing between 80,000–120,000 miles. Overheating can damage the aluminium block, so immediate attention is required.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW models use the M50/M52/M54?

acceptedAnswer.text: These engines power the E36 and E46 3 Series, E34 and E39 5 Series, E53 X5, Z3 and Z4 roadsters, and the E46 Compact.

mainEntity.name: How long does the BMW M50/M52/M54 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With regular maintenance, these engines routinely exceed 200,000 miles. Some M54B30 examples have reportedly surpassed 550,000 miles on the original engine. Cooling system preventative maintenance is the key to longevity.

hero.h1: BMW M50 / M52 / M54 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW M50, M52 and M54 are a family of naturally aspirated straight-six petrol engines — among the most durable engines BMW ever built. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

verdictRating.confidence: 90% confidence — based on UK specialist consensus and cross-referenced owner reports.

rows.score: 16 (Most failures are cheap, well-understood repairs (seals, sensors, cooling components))
20 (Parts are cheap and available from multiple UK suppliers same-day)
10 (Reconditioned cost vs. lowest host-vehicle value (~£2,500) = ~80% — above 75% threshold → 4, but adjusted due to enthusiast value retention)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The M50/M52/M54 family is legendary for durability — but cooling systems and oil leaks are the main weak points — buy with full service history and inspect the cooling system.
verdictRating.bestFor: Enthusiasts seeking a robust, tunable straight-six with proven longevity.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You're not prepared to proactively replace ageing cooling system components.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M50/M52/M54 typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE].
